content:

Member-only story

I can point to the exact project.

4 min read
Just now

--

Not the idea — the idea wasn’t special, just another weekend thing I was excited about on a Friday night the way I’d been excited about a dozen others.
What was different was what happened after, or more precisely, what didn’t happen.
The Same Beginning, Every Time
I opened a new folder, like always.
Initialized the repo, like always.
And then spent the next two hours doing the same thing I’d done the last time, and the time before that — deciding how routes should be organized, second-guessing an auth pattern I’d already used successfully on three other projects, rewriting an error handler I was fairly sure I’d written better once, in something I could no longer find.
None of it was hard.
All of it was familiar in the worst way, the way a chore is familiar.
By the time I actually got to the part of the project I cared about, the excitement from Friday night had quietly gone somewhere else.
Catching the Pattern While It Was Happening
I’d been through this exact evening before.
